briefdescription.para: \par \b Purpose\/ \: Define the TreeNode schema — a recursive Pydantic model for document hierarchical structure: each node has text, annotations, metadata, and child nodes.

detaileddescription.para: \par \b Scope\/ \: Document tree structure model — recursive node hierarchy. \par \b Input\/ \: None (standalone model, references Annotation, LineMetadata). \par \b Output\/ \: Pydantic BaseModel TreeNode with node_id, text, annotations, metadata, subparagraphs. \par \b Links\/ \: [USES_API(7): pydantic.BaseModel; READS_DATA_FROM(8): Annotation, LineMetadata] \par \b Invariants\/ \:

listitem.para: node_id is unique within the document tree (dot-separated path like "0.2.1").
subparagraphs is always a list (empty for leaf nodes).
The tree forms a valid DAG (no cycles). \par \b Rationale\/ \:

detaileddescription.para: Q: Why recursive TreeNode instead of flat list with parent references? A: Recursive structure mirrors document structure naturally and simplifies JSON serialization. Tree traversal is idiomatic in API consumers. \par \b Changes\/ \:
LAST_CHANGE: [v1.0.0 – Initial creation with semantic markup] \par \b Module Map\/ \:
CLASS 10[Recursive tree node for document hierarchy] => TreeNode \par \b Use Cases\/ \:
